skill.name: form-creator
skill.description: Create forms, validation schemas, and CRUD API endpoints. Use when building new data entry features or managing existing forms.
skill.summary: Generate typed React forms, Zod schemas, and authenticated CRUD API route patterns.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks and one weak cryptography pattern. Review found only documentation and code examples in SKILL.md and reference.md, with no executable commands, cryptography APIs, network access, or prompt injection. The static findings are false positives, so the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives
low_findings.description: Backtick detections in SKILL.md and reference.md are markdown inline code and fenced examples. The weak cryptography detection at SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting crypto API or algorithm evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain documentation, markdown code spans, and TypeScript examples only. No command execution, Ruby code, shell invocation, or cryptographic algorithm is present.

content.user_title: Create Typed Forms and CRUD APIs
content.value_statement: New data entry features often need matching forms, validation, and API routes. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to build consistent React forms with Zod validation and authenticated CRUD endpoints.
actual_capabilities: Defines Zod validation schemas and inferred TypeScript form types.
Creates React form components with react-hook-form and zodResolver.
Uses Shadcn form components, inputs, and loading state patterns.
Plans Next.js API routes for list, create, and update workflows.
Requires API route authorization with standard auth wrappers.
Encourages shared create and edit forms through initial data props.
limitations: Does not include complete generated source files inside the skill package.
Assumes the project already uses Zod, Shadcn, and react-hook-form.
Does not define database table schemas or migration steps.
Requires human review for authorization rules and data access logic.

use_cases.title: Add Admin Data Entry
use_cases.description: Build an authenticated admin form with validation, loading states, and create or edit behavior.

use_cases.title: Standardize Feature Forms
use_cases.description: Apply the same schema, component, and API route structure across multiple product features.

use_cases.title: Improve Form Reliability
use_cases.description: Replace loose form handling with typed Zod validation and predictable API error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Form
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new feature form.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the form-creator skill to create a typed form for a customer profile feature with name, email, and status fields.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request focused on validation quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the form-creator skill to define a Zod schema and React form for a billing plan with nullable and optional fields.

prompt_templates.title: Build Form and API
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a complete CRUD workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the form-creator skill to create the schema, reusable form component, and authenticated API routes for managing products.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Forms
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for consistency across an app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the form-creator skill to review existing settings forms and align them with shared schema, loading, and API patterns.

output_examples.input: Create a form for editing subscription plans.
output_examples.output: A plan form with typed validation, initial data support, submit handling, loading feedback, and matching protected API route guidance.
A refactor plan that separates schema validation, form component props, submit behavior, and API error handling.

output_examples.input: Add CRUD endpoints for a new admin resource.
output_examples.output: Route guidance for list, create, and update actions with authentication, schema validation, pagination, and structured error responses.

best_practices: Start with the validation schema before creating the form component.
Use the same form component for create and edit flows when possible.
Review every generated API route for authorization and data ownership.
anti_patterns: Do not use untyped form values or broad any types.
Do not skip server-side validation because the form validates client-side.
Do not publish API routes without the required authentication wrapper.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets React and Next.js projects that use Zod, react-hook-form, and Shadcn components.

faq.question: Can it create both create and edit forms?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends one reusable form component with initial data for edit flows.

faq.question: Does it handle API route security?
faq.answer: It instructs the assistant to wrap routes with required authentication helpers. You should still review authorization logic.

faq.question: Does it create database migrations?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on form schemas, form components, and CRUD API route patterns.

faq.question: Why were security findings dismissed?
faq.answer: The flagged backticks are markdown formatting and code examples. No executable shell command or weak crypto use was found.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
